articles.title: World Cup: Brazil downs Japan to book last 16 in dramatic fashion
articles.description: Substitute Gabriel Martinelli pounced deep in injury time as Brazil beat a stubborn Japan 2-1 on Monday to roll into the World Cup last 16. Arsenal winger Martinelli struck on 95 minutes in Houston to break Japanese hearts in the most dramatic fashion. The five-time champions will face either the Ivory Coast or Norway in New Jersey on Sunday after edging a captivating knockout encounter. READ: World Cup Round of 32: Brazil-Japan, Netherlands-Morocco Japan midfielder Kaishu Sano scored a classy solo goal after 29 minutes following a Brazil mistake to stun the huge numbers of fans decked out in yellow. [...]... Keep on reading: World Cup: Brazil downs Japan to book last 16 in dramatic fashion

articles.title: Trump: Housing affordability bill is 'a big yawn'
articles.description: STORY: :: Washington, D.C. / June 29, 2026 :: Trump says the bipartisan housing affordability bill is a 'big yawn' Reporter: "What are your plans for the housing bill, Mr. President? Are you...?" Trump: "I don't know. I think it's so unimportant by comparative, to the SAVE America Act. I think the Save America Act is exactly what it says is saving America from crooked elections. And the housing bill is a bill that can get approved. They worked on it long and hard. It's very bipartisan. That means the Democrats like it and it gets, maybe even, it's probably maybe more that way. They're getting things that I wouldn't necessarily agree to." :: Trump previously canceled the bill's signing to pressure Republicans to pass the 'SAVE America Act', a controversial voter ID bill Reporter: "But will you sign that housing bill?" Trump: "It hasn't been sent to me yet. It's coming, I understand, and then I'll make, then I'll make a.... Here's what I would like to sign. Much more than a bill, big deal. It's a yawn. Some people say it's wonderful. To me, compared to the Save America Act, just about everything is a big yawn." Speaking in the Oval Office on Monday, (June 29), Trump said he had yet to decide whether to sign the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which aims to boost housing supply ​through faster environmental reviews, new grants, and looser rules for prefabricated housing, but he stopped short of threatening ​a veto. House Speaker Mike Johnson, a Louisiana Republican, said on Sunday (June 28) that he would transmit the bill to the White House on Monday. From then, Trump has 10 days, excluding Sundays, to sign the bill or send it back to Congress. After that point, without his signature, it will become law.
